title: Deliciously Dense Thai Papaya Som Tum
description: A fresh and energizing Thai dish featuring shredded green papaya, tomatoes, and chilies, served in a dolsot stone bowl, inspired by the streets of Bangkok and Buddhist temple friendly.

ingredients.name: green papaya
ingredients.notes: use a julienne peeler to shred

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: vegetarian alternative: use soy sauce or tamari

instructions: Prepare the ingredients by shredding the green papaya, halving the cherry tomatoes, and chopping the peanuts and cilantro.
In a large mortar and pestle, pound the chilies and salt together until well combined.
Add the shredded papaya, halved tomatoes, and chopped peanuts to the mortar and pestle, and pound until the mixture is well combined and slightly bruised.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, lime juice, and palm sugar until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the papaya mixture and stir until everything is well coated.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the som tum mixture into a dolsot stone bowl.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve with steamed or toasted buns on the side.
